title: Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private)
text:
Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private) - SupportGoWhere
Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing): EASE (Private)
Share link
Provides subsidies to Singapore Citizen seniors living in private properties, for the installation of senior-friendly fittings.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
EASE (Private) will be in rolled out in phases from 1 April 2026, prioritising households with older seniors first
Apply via EASE (Private) portal
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support

EASE (Private) provides subsidies for the installation of senior-friendly fittings in private properties, to improve the mobility and safety of seniors within their homes. 

Eligible households will each receive $1,200 in vouchers that can be used to subsidise 75% of the cost of selected senior-friendly fittings purchased from appointed contractors. The household will only need to pay the remaining 25%. 

The seven senior-friendly fittings and their prices are as follows:  

 

	

Prices (after GST)

	

Prices after 75% subsidy




Single-Step Ramp 

	

$283.40 

	

$70.85 




Handrail 

	

$632.20 

	

$158.05 




Grab Bars  




First  set of 8 for combined toilet/bathroom or 10 pcs for separate toilet/bathroom 

	

$523.20 

	

$130.80 




Second set onwards 6 pcs 

	

$305.20 

	

$76.30 




Home Fire Alarm Device 

	

$119.90 

	

$29.98 




Bidet Spray 

	

$65.40 

	

$16.35 




Slip-Resistant Treatment to Existing Toilet Flooring* 




*The treatment is for size up to 3.5sqm. For non-standard bathroom sizes larger than 3.5 sqm, further assessment will be done during site survey by contractors.  

	

$239.80 

	

$59.95 




Shower Seat 

	

$305.20 

	

$76.30 

Who is this for?

EASE (Private) is available to private property households with at least one Singaporean household member aged:

65 years old and above; or  

Between 60 and 64 years old requiring assistance with at least one of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL)*

*ADL refers to daily self-care activities, including washing/bathing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility and transferring. 

Seniors aged 60 to 64 must submit a Functional Assessment Report endorsed by a qualified assessor, such as a doctor, registered nurse, or registered physiotherapist/occupational therapist.  

How to apply?

Applications for EASE (Private) will be rolled out in phases (based on age of seniors in the household in 2026):  

When to Apply 

	

Who Can Apply 




From 1 Apr 2026 onwards 

	

Households with seniors aged 80 and above 

Households with seniors aged 60 and above who require assistance with at least one of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL) 




From 1 Jul 2026 onwards 

	

Households with seniors aged 70 and above 




From 1 Oct 2026 onwards 

	

Households with seniors aged 65 and above 

Letters and SMSes will be progressively sent to eligible households to apply for EASE (Private). 

 

You can apply via BCA’s EASE (Private) Portal.




Seniors aged 60 to 64 who requires assistance for one or more of the Activities of Daily Living (ADL) must submit a Functional Assessment Report endorsed by a qualified assessor, such as a doctor, registered nurse or registered physiotherapist/occupational therapist.

Where can I find help?

If you require assistance with the application, you may visit any Community Centre (CC) or ServiceSG Centre. You may also call BCA's hotline  1800-342-5222.

 

If you wish to obtain hard copy EASE (Private) vouchers, you may visit any Community Centre islandwide and they would assist you to print the vouchers.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 19 May 2026
